care.tips: Native to: WIN GR, LEE BV, NIC OO, ECU, BZE BA

description: Polygala amarella, commonly known as dwarf milkwort or Kentish milkwort, is a plant of the family Polygalaceae. A European native, it grows on chalky grass land and limestone mountain pastures.

Polygala comosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. It is native to temperate Eurasia, including most of Europe, Turkey, the Caucasus, Siberia, parts of Central Asia, Xinjiang, and Mongolia.
Polygala monspeliaca is a species of annual herb in the family Polygalaceae. They have a self-supporting growth form and simple leaves. Individuals can grow to 5 cm.
Polygala serpyllifolia, the heath milkwort, is a European native perennial of heaths and grassy places.
Polygala vulgaris, known as the common milkwort, is a herbaceous perennial plant of the genus Polygala in the family Polygalaceae.
Chamaebuxus alpestris, synonyms including Polygaloides chamaebuxus, Polygala chamaebuxus, the shrubby milkwort, is an ornamental plant in the family Polygalaceae.
